decisions.title: partition customer database by user_id modulo 16
decisions.rationale: Modulo-16 sharding chosen over time-based partitioning to avoid hot-shard risk during write-heavy periods. Core assumption of uniform ID distribution refuted by incident INC-882: 2022 growth campaign IDs cluster on high-engagement accounts, causing 3.8× imbalance on partition 3.
evidence: modulo routing distributes writes evenly given uniform ID assignment; avoids time-based hot-shard risk (architecture decision, 2023-11-10)
INC-882 (2024-06-03): partition 3 reached 3.8× average row count; 2022 campaign IDs in 300–400K range generate 60–80% of writes

hypotheses.text: user account creation is approximately uniformly distributed across ID ranges, keeping modulo shards balanced over time
hypotheses.refuting_evidence: INC-882: growth campaign cohort (IDs 300–400K) concentrates 60–80% of write throughput on partition 3, causing 3.8× size imbalance
